summary: Backend (22/22 tests) and frontend e2e tests for BoltDashPi5 v.4Mag2026 all PASS. New System Updates feature is working end-to-end: /api/system/info, /api/system/check-updates, /api/system/scheduler/toggle, /api/system/updates/history all functional; Docker endpoints correctly return dockerAvailable:false without crashing; Login shows Ver.4Mag2026; Dashboard exposes the 6 tabs; UpdatesWidget on System Monitor shows the 4 fields and the 'Controlla' button triggers an apt check that returns 4 packages with estimated time; System Updates tab shows all 6 cards and 'Controlla Aggiornamenti' works. Found and fixed one real backend bug: scheduler/toggle endpoint missing async causing 500 (RuntimeError: no running event loop) when enabling scheduler.

fixed_during_testing.issue: Sync def endpoint called asyncio.create_task() from threadpool worker -> RuntimeError: no running event loop -> 500 when enabling scheduler
fixed_during_testing.fix: Changed `def toggle_scheduler` to `async def toggle_scheduler` in /app/backend/server.py (line 1227). Verified by re-running pytest (now passes).

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/system/check-updates returns packages_count + packages list + kernel_update_available
Backend: POST /api/system/scheduler/toggle flips boolean and persists; toggling back works (after fix)
Backend: destructive endpoints exist (GET returns 405, not 404) for /api/system/update, /api/system/kernel-update, /api/system/reboot
Frontend: Login page shows Ver.4Mag2026 string
Frontend: Login admin@dashboard.local works and lands on Dashboard
Frontend: 6 tabs present (System Monitor, Docker Backups, Containers Status, File Manager, System Updates, Settings)
Frontend: System Monitor renders updates-widget with 4 fields (Pacchetti, Kernel, Ultimo update, Auto-update)
Frontend: data-testid present: updates-widget, widget-check-btn, widget-open-btn, update-progress
Frontend: Clicking 'Controlla' in widget runs apt check and shows 'Stima tempo: ~1 min' with package list
Frontend: System Updates tab renders all 6 cards (Informazioni Sistema, Aggiornamenti Automatici, Aggiornamento Sistema, Aggiornamento Kernel, Riavvio Sistema, Cronologia)
Frontend: 'Controlla Aggiornamenti' button works, shows packages and estimated time
action_items: Validate the async fix to /api/system/scheduler/toggle is acceptable (already applied by testing agent, all tests green).
critical_code_review_comments: scheduler toggle endpoint was incorrectly defined as sync `def` while spawning asyncio tasks - generic anti-pattern; consider auditing other sync endpoints using asyncio.create_task or similar (none others spotted in scan).
/api/auth/me returns {user:{...}} while many other endpoints return resource flat. Inconsistent API shape - cosmetic, but may confuse clients.
/api/metrics/current returns {metrics:{...}} while frontend likely consumes this; consistent but worth documenting in API.
_run_apt uses 'sudo -n' which silently fails on non-Pi systems - already handled with returncode check; OK.
/api/system/reboot triggers actual subprocess Popen reboot in a fire-and-forget task - acceptable for the target Pi but ensure the deploy uses a dedicated user with limited sudoers.
server.py is ~1315 lines, approaching the 700-line guideline - consider splitting into routers/modules (auth, metrics, docker, files, settings, system).
seed_data_creation: None. Tests are read-mostly; scheduler toggled twice to restore prior state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_boltdash_api.py covers all endpoints incl. new System Updates. Reuse it as regression. Public URL: https://b8ea6577-e12a-4a4d-a283-d5c89eea915c.preview.emergentagent.com. NEVER POST to /api/system/update, /api/system/kernel-update, /api/system/reboot - they are destructive and run real apt/reboot in container. /api/system/check-updates is safe (read-only) and finds ~4 packages currently. Docker is unavailable in Emergent - endpoints must return dockerAvailable:false (already validated).
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/system/scheduler/toggle returned 500 when enabling: handler was sync `def` so FastAPI ran it in starlette's threadpool. The handler then called `asyncio.create_task(_scheduler_loop())` which requires a running event loop in the current thread; threadpool threads have none -> RuntimeError. Reproduced via pytest (test_scheduler_toggle_persists). Mitigation applied: declare handler as `async def` so it runs on the main event loop, where create_task works. Re-run tests: 22/22 pass.
